chief_complaint: An 11-month-old boy presented with 6 months of failure to thrive, progressive seizures, developmental regression, and aversion to solid foods.
history: The patient had a 6-month history of failure to thrive, progressive seizures, developmental regression, and aversion to solid foods. He also developed systemic symptoms including oral mucocutaneous lesions, neutropenia, and anemia. Initial diagnostic work-up included rapid whole exome sequencing (WES) and metabolic studies.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Investigations included an abnormal electroencephalogram, a thin corpus callosum, markedly elevated propionylcarnitine, plasma homocysteine, and methylmalonic acid, with normal vitamin B12, and maternal vitamin B12 undetectable.
correct_diagnosis: Maternal cobalamin deficiency causing infantile seizures and developmental regression.
differentials: Maternal cobalamin deficiency causing infantile seizures and developmental regression.
correct_ranking: Maternal cobalamin deficiency causing infantile seizures and developmental regression.
